depression.normal: This indicates that your mood has been relatively stable recently, with no significant signs of persistent sadness or loss of interest.
depression.mild: These mild symptoms may occasionally affect your energy and motivation, but they typically do not interfere significantly with daily functioning.
depression.moderate: At this level, feelings of low mood may cause persistent fatigue and reduced interest in activities, potentially affecting daily life and relationships.
depression.severe: Your score suggests notable emotional distress that may be significantly affecting your quality of life. Reaching out to a mental health professional is recommended.

anxiety.normal: This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
anxiety.mild: Mild tension or worry is common in everyday life. Simple self-regulation techniques can often help manage these feelings.
anxiety.moderate: At this level, you may find it difficult to relax, experience frequent worry, and notice effects on sleep or concentration.
anxiety.severe: Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.

stress.normal: This indicates that your current stress levels are manageable and you are coping well with daily challenges.
stress.mild: Mild stress is a normal part of life. Learning stress management techniques can help you handle it more effectively.
stress.moderate: At this level, stress may cause persistent tension, mood fluctuations, and reduced work efficiency or rest quality.
stress.severe: Your stress level is elevated and may be significantly affecting your physical and mental health. Seeking support and making adjustments is recommended.

trauma.normal: This indicates that you are not currently showing significant post-traumatic stress reactions, and your psychological recovery is progressing well.
trauma.mild: Mild trauma-related reactions are normal after experiencing stressful events and typically diminish over time.
trauma.moderate: At this level, trauma responses may include flashbacks, avoidance behaviors, or emotional numbness that affect daily life and relationships.
trauma.severe: Your trauma-related reactions are quite pronounced and may be significantly impacting your functioning. Professional trauma-focused support is recommended.

sleep.normal: This indicates that your sleep quality and patterns are in good condition, allowing you to get adequate rest.
sleep.mild: Mild sleep difficulties are quite common. Adjusting your sleep habits and environment can often bring improvement.
sleep.moderate: At this level, sleep problems may cause daytime fatigue and affect your concentration and emotional stability.
sleep.severe: Your sleep difficulties are significant and may be considerably affecting your daytime functioning and overall health. Consulting a sleep specialist is recommended.

substance.normal: This indicates that your current substance-use-related risk is low and within a healthy range.
substance.mild: A mild risk level suggests it may be helpful to pay attention to your usage patterns and maintain self-awareness.
substance.moderate: Your score suggests that substance use may already be affecting certain areas of your life. Further evaluation is recommended.
substance.severe: Your score indicates elevated substance-use-related risk. Speaking with a professional for personalized support and guidance is recommended.

eating.normal: This indicates that your eating attitudes and behaviors are within a healthy range, and your relationship with food is generally positive.
eating.mild: Mild eating-related concerns are fairly common. Paying attention to your eating patterns and feelings can help maintain a healthy relationship with food.
eating.moderate: Your score suggests that eating attitudes or behaviors may be affecting your physical and emotional well-being to some extent.
eating.severe: Your eating-related concerns are notable and may be significantly affecting your nutritional status and mental health. Consulting a professional is recommended.

wellbeing.normal: This indicates that your current sense of well-being and life satisfaction is relatively low. Focusing on self-care and lifestyle adjustments may be beneficial.
wellbeing.mild: Your well-being is at a moderate level. Increasing positive experiences and social connections may help improve your overall sense of fulfillment.
wellbeing.moderate: This level reflects a generally positive psychological state, where you can experience meaning and satisfaction in most aspects of life.
wellbeing.severe: Your well-being and positive experiences are at a high level, reflecting strong psychological health and life adaptation.

personality.normal: This indicates that this personality dimension falls within the typical range commonly seen in the general population.
personality.mild: This level of trait expression is slightly above average but within the normal range of individual variation.
personality.moderate: This trait is notably expressed and may influence your interpersonal interactions and self-perception in certain situations.
personality.severe: This personality dimension score is elevated and may significantly influence your behavior patterns and relationships across various contexts. Further exploration is recommended.

behavioral.normal: This indicates that the relevant behavioral characteristics are within the normal range with no significant functional impact.
behavioral.mild: Mild behavioral differences are common. Understanding your own patterns can help you adapt more effectively to different environments.
behavioral.moderate: At this level, behavioral characteristics may have some impact on learning, work, or social interactions. Understanding and adapting strategies can be helpful.
behavioral.severe: Your behavioral characteristic scores are elevated and may be considerably affecting daily functioning. Professional assessment and support is recommended.

somatic.normal: This indicates that you are currently experiencing minimal physical discomfort, with somatic symptoms within the normal range.
somatic.mild: Mild physical discomfort is common. Rest and healthy lifestyle habits can often help alleviate these symptoms.
somatic.moderate: At this level, physical symptoms may cause discomfort and affect your daily activities and emotional state.
somatic.severe: Your somatic symptom scores are elevated, and these discomforts may be significantly affecting your quality of life. Both medical and psychological evaluation is recommended.

social.normal: This indicates that your social functioning and relationships are in good condition, maintaining satisfactory social connections.
social.mild: Mild social concerns are common in everyday life. Expanding social activities and expressing your feelings may be helpful.
social.moderate: At this level, social difficulties may cause feelings of isolation or discomfort in interpersonal interactions, affecting life satisfaction.
social.severe: Your social concerns are notable and may be significantly affecting your relationships and quality of life. Professional support is recommended.

general.normal: This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
general.mild: Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
general.moderate: At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
general.severe: Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
